summary: This week's AI news highlights significant advancements in AI technology, including updates to Alexa Plus and Claude's voice mode. Companies like AMD and NVIDIA are investing heavily in AI, while lawmakers are considering regulations to mitigate potential risks.
insight: The increasing investment in AI by major companies like AMD and NVIDIA underscores the growing importance of AI in various industries, from smart home devices to cloud computing.
highlights: AMD is challenging NVIDIA with its Helios AI rack-scale system
OpenAI is making ChatGPT Health available to all US users
Google's massive AI spending has led to a negative cash flow quarter

articles.title: AMD takes on Nvidia with its Helios AI rack-scale system
articles.description: AMD is challenging its chipmaker rival with a new rack-scale system that will start shipping to customers later this year.

articles.title: OpenAI makes ChatGPT Health available to all US users
articles.description: Users can also integrate their personal data from services like Apple Health, Function, and MyFitnessPal.

articles.title: Alexa Plus is getting an AI update to handle more complicated instructions
articles.description: Amazon is launching an update to its Alexa Plus assistant that will allow it to connect to smart home devices in new ways. With the update, which is currently in preview, Alexa Plu

articles.title: Google just had its first negative cash flow quarter due to massive AI spending
articles.description: Google continues to report big quarterly revenue, but its AI spending has skyrocketed.

articles.title: Anthropic updates Claude voice mode with more capable models
articles.description: Claude's new voice model will let you reschedule your meeting or draft an email.

articles.title: AI Kill Switch Act would let Trump admin order shutdown of rogue AI systems
articles.description: Bill would let Homeland Security chief decide when an AI should be shut down.

articles.title: AI chip startup Etched defies skeptics, hits $10.3B valuation from big-name investors
articles.description: Etched, founded by three Harvard dropouts, has created new chips and memory components that speed up inference on any AI model -- no GPUs required, it says.

articles.title: AMD commits up to $5 billion to  Anthropic

articles.title: AegisAI, founded by former Google security execs, lands $36M to stop AI-driven spear phishing

articles.title: Travis Kalanick’s robotics company raises $1.7B, led by a16z
articles.description: Uber is also investing in Travis Kalanick's company Atoms, which has made gauzy claims about using industrial AI to modernize the world.

articles.title: How AI helps scientists design the next generation of medicines

articles.title: NVIDIA Open Sources First GPU-Accelerated Medical Physics Simulation Framework
